title: Customize chart widget colors
excerpt: I have two charts on my dashboard, one looks at value split up by project type, the other looks at time again split up by project type. I really wish I could adjust the colors because for some reason Monday.com assigns r…

posts.author: aruggirello (Annina Ruggirello)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have two charts on my dashboard, one looks at value split up by project type, the other looks at time again split up by project type. I really wish I could adjust the colors because for some reason Monday.com assigns random colors and the SAME PROJECT TYPE will have two different colors. (see screenshot). Level 3 on the top chart is blue but then on the bottom chart level 2 is blue
My team likes to look at these two items together and compare the time it takes for a project type vs the revenue it brings it and having the colors differ makes it very confusing.

image603×681 28.3 KB

posts.author: robgibbs (Rob Gibbs)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 I have a similar problem but with other graphs like Bar and Line graphs that I’d like to stabalize the colors. Remember, we use “shapes and colors” for executives, so this is their most likely interaction with Monday.com and they pay for it…so please bump this up the feature list!!!

posts.author: Desw (Des Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same need, we would be able to highlight “most” important data (at a glance) in dashboards to our audience

posts.author: cperazza (Calebe Perazza)
posts.cookedHtml: YES - please let us manage the chart colour!!!

posts.author: mbole (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, please add functionality to change chart colours manually.

posts.author: jbeagle (John)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a must have for chart functionality, at the very least allow us to assign colors to number columns like we can with status/labels because those colors seem to carry over into charts anyway.

posts.author: Simon.H (Simon Hart)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
This is the first issue I’ve run into trying to adopt Monday as our go-to team management tool.
Because the colors are inconsistent, whatever data the viewer is looking at has to be re-interpreted every time they view it - we can’t use basic graph fundamentals like green = good, red = bad.

Having color choice for benchmark lines but not for the charts themselves feels like a glaring oversight or poor prioritization.

posts.author: ColbyPeterson (Colby Peterson)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

I love Dashboards but we really need to be able to set our own colors. I want each chart color to represent an item consistently across one dashboard. It melts my brain to have to readjust every time I go from chart to chart on the same Dashboard.

posts.author: Joshing (Joshua)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here. I have colors set for other charts and the auto-color in the pie chart makes it difficult to tell them apart across the board. Please add feature for us to control the color scheme of the pie chart.
Thank you!

posts.author: egronberger (Erik Gronberger)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 here, it is really important to change the colours manually

posts.author: LotusEllen (A. Ellen Moore)
posts.cookedHtml: We are thinking about adopting Monday.com and not being able to change colors in bar charts could be a deal-breaker. For example, if a bar chart shows a negative number (bar below the x axis), really want to color it red!

posts.author: avicamhi (Avi Camhi)
posts.cookedHtml: It is essential to be able to change chart colors! Please add such simple functionality

posts.author: Dana_Shemer (Dana S)
posts.cookedHtml: +1. Very basic functionality that exists on our project management platforms

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml: As a user of the platform for over 3 years, this makes a TON of sense. You got my vote!

posts.author: ktomasko (Kristyn Tomasko)
posts.cookedHtml: I would very much appreciate the ability to assign colors in the dashboard areas. We use a capacity planning calendar segmented by team member, but each time we access the dashboard the colors change. This inconsistency is not desirable - please open the ability to assign permanent colors within dashboard widgets.

posts.author: Joelburden (Joel Burden)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. We need custom colour and font choices for dashboards.

posts.author: Nikki (Nikki Clemmons)
posts.cookedHtml: Please monday.com simple Excel color functionality is a must for charts.

posts.author: Sam-APC (Sam Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: +1. Very basic and essential functionality. Please add this

posts.author: r.sironneau (Sironneau)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 need this feature !

posts.author: RENEE.L (RENEE)
posts.cookedHtml:
same need here.
and it’s even weird that, the color of bars changes everytime I check on the dashboard. so it’s not stay the same for the same chart.
really couldn’t understand why this takes so long time to resolve this issue.
please let us know when we can expect a solution. thx.
@monday-team @CXS

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. I have deleted and re-added columns, created formula columns, etc just to make the colors match. I have been mostly successful, but still a lot work. Short of having the ability to control the numbers, can we have an explanation of how the colors are chosen so we can control column selection to align with the colors we want?

posts.author: Tracy (Tracy Emma Pontin)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve just requested the same thing as i hadn’t found your post. this is such an obvious requirement. i don’t understand why it’s not available yet.

posts.author: MattJ (Matthew Jackson)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this functionality. It is an important way to communicate infromation.

posts.author: sjfizz (Shehan Jesudason)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this functionality

posts.author: Alexandra (Alexandra Hassell)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 this is such a simple and obvious feature but MADDENING when you realize you don’t have it!

posts.author: abouthillier (Alex Bouthillier)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just discovered this missing feature today, and I’m amazed it doesn’t exist. My pie chart is pulling items from another connected board and the colors don’t match the color of the group that they’re coming from, which is maddening.

I want time tracking issues fixed first, but this next!

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml: Would also love to see this happen! It’s essential when trying to compare information and understand what we are viewing.

posts.author: Sophie_Tryve (Sophie Uijterschout)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 please add this feature, being able to adjust colours in general through a hex code instead of the pre-set colours in boards and dashboards would make a huge difference

posts.author: KWagar (Keith Wagar)
posts.cookedHtml: PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE Add this feature ASAP. I am trying to show the RAG status of projects (Red, Amber and Green) but it’s very confusing when your label show projects in Red but the pie chart is showing them in blue or green and same for the others…

posts.author: awilding (Abner Wilding)
posts.cookedHtml: Super important feature, I could insert charts in presentations (screenshot) if I could control their colors. As it is I can’t use the dashboard at all.

posts.author: codonnell (Chris O)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can’t believe you can’t change the colors! We want to show a pass/fail chart with green and red. Is super confusing to have yellow and blue.

Please roll this out soon!! I can’t use dashboards because of this, will probably try pull data into google sheets or something, so painful. Makes we want to change off Monday.com to another product.

posts.author: WorkingWheels (Lara )
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely this. It’s really surprising to me that we can’t edit the chart colors. It’s a necessary functionality.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, we desperately need ability to change the color schema to match our company’s branding requirements.

posts.author: FredrikR (Fredrik Remahl)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 doesn’t seem like a big feature update and hopefully Monday.com can have this implemented sooner than later

posts.author: higheredpmo (Sandy Bury)
posts.cookedHtml: Please allow color customization of charts/dashboards. We prefer subdued colors - only complaint about Monday is these elementary school chart colors. Need to customize presentations for our university colors.

posts.author: adowell (A. Dowell)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to have the ability to set colors of reports/dashboards manually. I work with several team members who are different degrees of color blind. They need severe contrasting colors to be able to tell them apart. The colors that the reports pull automatically are often way too close for them to differentiate.

posts.author: higheredpmo (Sandy Bury)
posts.cookedHtml: Please make dashboard charts and graphs customizable with color edits/theme choices. We need to tailor to our university’s color scheme. Thank you.

posts.author: DemiA (Demi Adesanya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Need the feature to be able to keep items of the same name consistent across multiple charts on the same dashboard. Example I would like "Distribution Brands to remain Blue Across the chart

posts.author: Izobelle (Isabelle)

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Thank you for all the thoughtful feedback. The product team is looking into the different development and design requirements to better understand the effort needed to add the ability to customize chart widget colors on boards. At the moment, we do not have a a timeline for this feature, but are planning on starting to develop this feature in the future.

What it means when a feature request is "Under consideration" Feature requests
If a feature is marked as “under consideration” it means that while we understand the need for this feature, at the moment, this feature is not planned in our upcoming roadmap due to other priorities and team capacity. We are looking into this feature and are considering adding it to our roadmaps in the future. When thinking about the upcoming roadmap, our team needs to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize new features, improve old ones, and ensure overall platform quality. Please b…

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s disappointing that this feature request is outstanding for 3 years.

posts.author: Sandkatze (Patrick Straten)
posts.cookedHtml: This really needs to be made a feedback, in order to have the dashboards be useful for any review.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: At the moment, the team is finalizing the upcoming roadmap, which includes customizing chart widget colors. While we do not have an exact timeline of when the feature will be available in 2024, our community will be the first to hear know when the timeline is finalized so make sure to turn your notifications on the post. Here is how: How to turn on notifications for a post

posts.author: MZAWI (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature request is almost three years old, for something that should’ve been rolled out Day 1. At the very least, do you have a designer that can create a color pallet easy on the eyes? I’m forced to send my C-Suite gaudy reports with a Miami Vice/80’s vibe.

Embarrassing!

posts.author: adrwh (adrwh)
posts.cookedHtml: The beauty of Monday is its simplicity, colour selection should be baked in from the very beginning. How are we going with this Monday?

posts.author: Katinal2 (KATINA EHRMAN)
posts.cookedHtml: do we have a status of when this will be happening in 2024? my leadership team would like to know to see if we are going to wait or look for another product. Thanks

posts.author: TGoon (Taylor Goon)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

I developed a report for a board meeting and have been requested to make colors match each item on all graphs. I shared that I cannot and was told it is too confusing to share as is (and I agreed). I was then requested to find another way.

I love this tool, but this is a massive disappointment on what is perceived to many as a “why wouldn’t you be able to” issue and makes me look incompetent when presenting reports.

@monday.com any ETA on this one would be greatly appreciated.

posts.author: AWallace0013 (Amanda Wallace)
posts.cookedHtml: Does the dev team have an ETA on this? Between this and other limitations (ineffective gantt chart, lack of customization for exports, etc.) we have a pretty long con list that is leaning towards us not renewing and transitioning to a different product.

posts.author: jessadhay (Jessa Dhay Buenaobra)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this feature request? It’s been 3 years already and this basic function is not available yet.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
HI! I am excited to share that this feature is coming soon! You will see be able to customize chart widget colors. The team is planning start releasing this feature in Q3 along with reordering the chart columns.

Thanks for all your feedback and helping our team bring this feature to life!

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
When I create charts colours are automatically assigned but I have no way of changing that or controlling which are assigned to which option.

I use charts to compare leads and sales stacked by leadsource (Dropdown field). But on all the charts the leadsources all have different colours which is really confusing. Each widget does have a key but naturally if you put 2 charts next to eachother and the colours all contradict it its really tricky for the end user to see quickly what the chart is showing them. Once you associate one colour with one leadsource your brain fills in the gaps and assumes the same applies on others. Would be great if you could select or control the colours for each option.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, Amy here! We’re very excited to share that customizable chart colors are officially here! This long-requested feature is now rolling out gradually, allowing you to tailor your chart colors across all your dashboards, board views, docs, and item pages, without any extra steps or new engines. To access this feature, make sure you’ve upgraded to the new dashboard engine (you can do this by clicking the three-dot menu at the top right of your dashboard and selecting “Upgrade to new dashboard engine”).

What’s the difference between Pro and ENT? In Pro, color customization is limited to any Monday color. In Enterprise, you’ll have access to a full color picker, allowing you to use your brand colors.

Where will you be able to customize your chart colors? Any dashboard, any board view, any doc, and any item page (as long as you’re in Pro or Enterprise tiers).

Creating products you love starts with your feedback, and we’re excited to bring this feature to life because of you. Thank you for helping us prioritize this - we can’t wait to see how you bring your data to life!

posts.author: wct (Luke)
posts.cookedHtml:
Someone from live support helped me solve this issue. If the “Customize” tab does not show up, click the three dots in the right hand corner and select “Upgrade to new dashboard engine.” It should refresh the page and then show the “Customize” option.

image442×439 16.8 KB
